name: EBNER RES (SUSAN)
address: 2348 NW TOWER ROCK RD, BEND, OR 97703

fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency responder access and operations at your property could be achieved by addressing the current dense vegetation, especially around the main building and landscaped areas. Thinning or pruning this vegetation may enhance visibility and movement, facilitating safer navigation for responders. Incorporating additional or improved access routes to the rear of the property could also support more flexible and effective emergency operations, allowing for multiple entry points and better maneuverability for emergency vehicles. By orienting the front driveway to accommodate clear and unrestricted passage, responders would benefit from easier access and egress, ensuring prompt and efficient response activities.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by managing the vegetation adjacent to the structure can reduce potential exposure and help in minimizing fuel continuity. Increasing the separation between tree clusters and structures could further aid in reducing fire spread. Regular maintenance of the vegetated perimeter and vegetated access route is crucial to support effective firefighter access and suppression operations. Efforts to thin the dense tree canopy and create open terrain where feasible will preserve pathways for safe access and may support long-term resilience. Additionally, improving or maintaining the limited rear access path could be vital for firefighter mobility and effective operational responses. By managing vegetation concentrations and ensuring clear access routes, the property could bolster its preparedness against wildfire exposure while fostering a strengthened defensible space around structures.
